tools.name: index_file
tools.description: Index a single file within an existing index. Faster than index_folder for surgical updates after editing a file. The file must be within an already-indexed folder's source_root. Can also add new files not yet in the index.

path.description: Absolute path to the file to index

use_ai_summaries.description: Use AI to generate symbol summaries. Supports Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, MiniMax, and GLM-5 via env vars. When false, uses docstrings or signature fallback.

tools.name: list_repos
tools.description: List all indexed repositories. START HERE before using Grep/Read/search tools — check if the project is already indexed, then use search_symbols / get_symbol_source instead of native file reads. If jcodemunch tools appear as deferred in your tool list, call ToolSearch to load their schemas first.

tools.name: get_watch_status
tools.description: Report watch-all daemon coverage: every locally-indexed repo, each repo's staleness / reindex-in-progress state, and the OS-level service status. Call before relying on index freshness when you suspect files may have changed since the last index.

tools.name: resolve_repo
tools.description: Resolve a filesystem path to its indexed repo identifier. O(1) lookup — faster than list_repos for finding a single repo. Accepts repo root, worktree, subdirectory, or file path.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path (repo root, worktree, subdirectory, or file)

tools.name: get_symbol_source
tools.description: Get full source of one symbol (symbol_id → flat object) or many (symbol_ids[] → {symbols, errors}). Supports verify, context_lines, and fqn (PHP FQN via PSR-4).

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

symbol_id.description: Single symbol ID — returns flat symbol object

verify.description: Verify content hash matches stored hash (detects source drift)

context_lines.description: Number of lines before/after symbol to include for context

fqn.description: PHP fully-qualified class name (e.g. 'App\Models\User'). Resolves to symbol_id via PSR-4. Alternative to symbol_id.

tools.name: get_file_content
tools.description: Get cached source for a file, optionally sliced to a line range.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

file_path.description: Path to the file within the repository (e.g., 'src/main.py')

tools.name: search_symbols
tools.description: Search for symbols matching a query across the entire indexed repository. Returns matches with signatures and summaries.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

query.description: Search query (matches symbol names, signatures, summaries, docstrings)

file_pattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter files (e.g., 'src/**/*.py')

decorator.description: Optional filter: only return symbols with this decorator (case-insensitive substring match, e.g. 'route', 'property', 'Deprecated')

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (ignored when token_budget is set)

token_budget.description: Token budget cap. When set, results are sorted by score and greedily packed until the budget is exhausted. Overrides max_results. Reports token_budget, tokens_used, and tokens_remaining in _meta.

detail_level.description: Controls result verbosity. 'compact' returns id/name/kind/file/line only (~15 tokens each, best for broad discovery). 'standard' returns signatures and summaries (default). 'full' inlines source code, docstring, and end_line — equivalent to search + get_symbol in one call.

debug.description: When true, each result includes a score_breakdown showing per-field scoring contributions (name_exact, name_contains, name_word_overlap, signature_phrase, signature_word_overlap, summary_phrase, summary_word_overlap, keywords, docstring_word_overlap). Also adds candidates_scored to _meta.

fuzzy.description: Enable fuzzy matching. When true, uses trigram overlap + Levenshtein distance as fallback when BM25 scores are low. Fuzzy results include match_type, fuzzy_similarity, and edit_distance fields.

fuzzy_threshold.description: Minimum Jaccard trigram similarity (0.0–1.0) for fuzzy candidates. Lower values surface more candidates. Default 0.4.

max_edit_distance.description: Maximum Levenshtein distance for direct name matching (catches typos). Default 2.

sort_by.description: Ranking strategy. 'relevance' (default) = BM25 text match. 'centrality' = filter by query, rank by PageRank. 'combined' = BM25 + PageRank weighted.

semantic.description: Enable semantic (embedding-based) search. Requires an embedding provider: JCODEMUNCH_EMBED_MODEL (sentence-transformers), GOOGLE_API_KEY+GOOGLE_EMBED_MODEL (Gemini), or OPENAI_API_KEY+OPENAI_EMBED_MODEL (OpenAI). When false (default) there is zero performance impact.

semantic_weight.description: Weight for semantic score in hybrid BM25+embedding ranking (0.0–1.0). BM25 receives 1-weight. Default 0.5. Set to 0.0 for identical results to pure BM25; set to 1.0 for pure semantic.

semantic_only.description: Skip BM25 entirely and rank solely by embedding cosine similarity. Implies semantic=true.

fusion.description: Enable multi-signal fusion (Weighted Reciprocal Rank) across lexical, structural, similarity, and identity channels. Produces higher-quality ranking than linear score addition. When True, sort_by is ignored.

fqn.description: PHP fully-qualified class name (e.g. 'App\Models\User'). Resolves via PSR-4 and uses the class name as query. Alternative to query.

tools.name: invalidate_cache
tools.description: Delete the index and cached files for a repository. Forces a full re-index on next index_repo or index_folder call.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

tools.name: search_text
tools.description: Full-text search across indexed file contents. Useful when symbol search misses (e.g., string literals, comments, config values). Supports regex (is_regex=true) and context lines around matches (context_lines=N, like grep -C).

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

query.description: Text to search for. Case-insensitive substring by default. Set is_regex=true for full regex (e.g. 'estimateToken|tokenEstimat|\.length.*0\.25').

is_regex.description: When true, treat query as a Python regex (re.search, case-insensitive). Supports alternation (|), character classes, lookaheads, etc.

file_pattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter files (e.g., '*.py')

max_results.description: Maximum number of matching lines to return

context_lines.description: Lines of context to include before and after each match (like grep -C N). Essential for understanding code around matches.

tools.name: find_importers
tools.description: Find all files that import a given file. Answers 'what uses this file?'. has_importers=false on a result means that importer is itself unreachable (dead code chain). Supports dbt {{ ref() }} edges. Use file_paths for batch queries. Set cross_repo=true to also find importers in other indexed repos.

repo.description: Repository identifier

file_path.description: Target file path within the repo (e.g. 'src/features/intake/IntakeService.js'). Use for single-file queries. Cannot be used together with file_paths.

file_paths.description: List of target file paths for batch queries. Returns a results array. Cannot be used together with file_path.

cross_repo.description: When true, also search other indexed repos for cross-repo importers. Default: false (or JCODEMUNCH_CROSS_REPO_DEFAULT env var).

tools.name: find_references
tools.description: Find all files that import or reference an identifier. Answers 'where is this used?'. Supports dbt {{ ref() }} edges. Use identifiers for batch queries. Set include_call_chain=true to also see which symbols in each file call the identifier.

repo.description: Repository identifier

identifier.description: Symbol or module name to search for (e.g. 'bulkImport', 'IntakeService'). Use for single-identifier queries. Cannot be used together with identifiers.

identifiers.description: List of symbol or module names to search for (batch mode). Returns a results array. Cannot be used together with identifier.

include_call_chain.description: When true (singular mode only), each reference entry includes calling_symbols: symbols in that file whose bodies mention the identifier. Default false.

tools.name: check_references
tools.description: Check if an identifier is referenced anywhere: imports + file content. Combines find_references and search_text into one call. Returns is_referenced (bool) for quick dead-code detection. Accepts multiple identifiers in one call via identifiers param.

repo.description: Repository identifier

identifiers.description: Multiple identifiers to check in one call. Returns grouped results.

search_content.description: Also search file contents (not just imports). Set false for fast import-only check.

max_content_results.description: Max files to return per identifier for content search.

tools.name: search_columns
tools.description: Search column metadata across indexed models. Works with any ecosystem provider that emits column data (dbt, SQLMesh, database catalogs, etc.). Returns model name, file path, column name, and description. Use instead of grep/search_text for column discovery — 77% fewer tokens.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

query.description: Search query (matches column names and descriptions)

model_pattern.description: Optional glob to filter by model name (e.g., 'fact_*', 'dim_provider')

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

tools.name: get_context_bundle
tools.description: Get full source + imports for one or more symbols in one call. Multi-symbol bundles deduplicate shared imports. Set token_budget to cap response size; use budget_strategy to control what's kept. Supports fqn (PHP FQN via PSR-4) as alternative to symbol_id.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

symbol_id.description: Single symbol ID (backward-compatible). Use symbol_ids for multi-symbol bundles.

symbol_ids.description: List of symbol IDs for a multi-symbol bundle. Imports are deduplicated across symbols that share a file.

include_callers.description: When true, each symbol entry includes a 'callers' list of files that directly import its defining file.

output_format.description: 'json' (default) or 'markdown' — markdown renders a paste-ready document with imports, docstrings, and source blocks.

token_budget.description: Max tokens to return. When set, symbols are ranked and trimmed to fit. Uses budget_strategy to prioritize.

budget_strategy.description: 'most_relevant' (default) ranks by file centrality (import in-degree). 'core_first' keeps the primary symbol first, ranks rest by centrality. 'compact' strips source bodies — returns signatures only.

include_budget_report.description: When true, include a 'budget_report' field showing tokens used, symbols included/excluded, and strategy applied.

fqn.description: PHP fully-qualified class name (e.g. 'App\Models\User'). Resolves to symbol_id via PSR-4. Alternative to symbol_id.

tools.name: get_session_context
tools.description: Get the current session context — files accessed, searches performed, and edits registered during this MCP session. Use to avoid re-reading the same files.

max_files.description: Maximum number of files to return in files_accessed.

max_queries.description: Maximum number of queries to return in recent_searches.

tools.name: get_session_snapshot
tools.description: Get a compact session snapshot for context continuity. Returns a ~200 token markdown summary of files explored, edits made, searches performed, and dead ends. Designed for injection after context compaction to restore session orientation.

include_negative_evidence.description: Include dead-end searches (negative evidence) in snapshot.

tools.name: register_edit
tools.description: Register file edits to invalidate caches. Call after editing files to clear BM25 cache and search result cache for the repo.

repo.description: Repository identifier.

file_paths.description: List of file paths that were edited.

reindex.description: If True, also reindex the files.

tools.name: get_symbol_diff
tools.description: Diff symbol sets between two indexed snapshots. Shows added, removed, and changed symbols. Branch workflow: index branch A as repo-main, index branch B as repo-feature, then diff.

tools.name: get_related_symbols
tools.description: Find symbols related to a given symbol using heuristic clustering: same-file co-location (weight 3), shared importers (weight 1.5), and name-token overlap (weight 0.5/token). Useful for discovering what else to read when exploring an unfamiliar codebase.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

symbol_id.description: ID of the symbol to find relatives for

tools.name: get_call_hierarchy
tools.description: Return incoming callers and outgoing callees for a symbol, N levels deep. Uses AST-derived call detection: callers = symbols in importing files that mention this name; callees = imported symbols mentioned in this symbol's body. Useful for understanding how a symbol fits into the call graph before refactoring. For a 'what breaks if I delete this?' answer, use get_impact_preview instead.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

symbol_id.description: Symbol name or full ID to analyse. Use search_symbols to find IDs.

direction.description: 'callers' = who calls this symbol; 'callees' = what this symbol calls; 'both' (default).

tools.name: get_symbol_provenance
tools.description: Trace the complete authorship lineage and evolution narrative of a symbol through git history. Returns every commit that touched the symbol (or its file), classified into semantic categories (creation, bugfix, refactor, feature, perf, rename, revert, etc.) with extracted commit intent. Includes a human-readable narrative summarising who created it, why, how it evolved, and how volatile it is. Use before refactoring unfamiliar code to understand the 'why' behind it. Requires a locally indexed repo (index_folder).

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

symbol.description: Symbol name or full ID as returned by search_symbols.

tools.name: check_rename_safe
tools.description: Check whether renaming a symbol to a new name would cause name collisions. Scans the symbol's own file and every file that imports it, looking for an existing symbol with the proposed new name. Returns safe=true when no collisions are found. Run this before any rename/refactor to avoid silent breakage. For a full rename plan with edits, use plan_refactoring.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

symbol_id.description: Symbol ID to rename (e.g. 'src/utils.py::helper#function'). Bare name accepted when unambiguous.

new_name.description: Proposed new symbol name (not a full ID, just the name).

tools.name: get_dead_code_v2
tools.description: Find likely-dead functions and methods using three independent evidence signals: (1) the symbol's file is not reachable from any entry point via the import graph, (2) no indexed symbol calls this symbol in the call graph, (3) the symbol name is not re-exported from any __init__ or barrel file. Each result includes a confidence score (0.33 = 1 signal, 0.67 = 2 signals, 1.0 = all 3). More reliable than single-signal dead-code detection. Use min_confidence=0.67 for high-confidence results only.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

min_confidence.description: Minimum confidence threshold 0.0–1.0 (default 0.5 = at least 2/3 signals).

include_tests.description: Include test files in analysis (default false).

tools.name: get_symbol_complexity
tools.description: Return cyclomatic complexity, nesting depth, and parameter count for a single symbol. Complexity data is stored at index time (requires jcodemunch-mcp >= 1.16 / INDEX_VERSION 7). assessment field: 'low' (1-4), 'medium' (5-10), 'high' (11+). Re-index the repo if all metrics show 0 (pre-1.16 index).

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

symbol_id.description: Full symbol ID as returned by search_symbols or get_file_outline.

tools.name: get_untested_symbols
tools.description: Find functions and methods with no evidence of being exercised by any test file. Uses import-graph reachability + name matching (AST call_references when available, word-boundary text heuristic as fallback). Returns symbols classified as 'unreached' (no test file imports the source file) or 'imported_not_called' (test imports the module but no test references this specific function). This is heuristic reachability, NOT runtime coverage — it answers 'does any test reference this symbol?' rather than 'what % of lines are covered.' Use after get_repo_health for a deeper quality picture.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

file_pattern.description: Optional glob to narrow which source files are analysed (e.g. 'src/**/*.py').

tools.name: search_ast
tools.description: Cross-language AST pattern matching. Finds structural code patterns across all 70+ indexed languages using a single query — no need to know language-specific AST node types. Two modes: (1) preset anti-patterns (empty_catch, bare_except, deeply_nested, nested_loops, god_function, eval_exec, hardcoded_secret, todo_fixme, magic_number, reassigned_param), or (2) custom mini-DSL (call:*.unwrap, string:/password/i, comment:/TODO/i, nesting:5+, loops:3+, lines:80+). Use category='all' to run every preset at once, or category='security'/'error_handling'/'complexity'/'performance'/'maintenance' for a focused scan. Every match is attributed to its enclosing indexed symbol with complexity metadata. Requires a locally indexed repo.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

pattern.description: Preset name (empty_catch, bare_except, deeply_nested, nested_loops, god_function, eval_exec, hardcoded_secret, todo_fixme, magic_number, reassigned_param) or custom query (call:NAME, string:/REGEX/i, comment:/REGEX/i, nesting:N+, loops:N+, lines:N+). Mutually exclusive with category.

category.description: Run all presets in a category: security, error_handling, complexity, performance, maintenance, or all.

tools.name: find_dead_code
tools.description: Find dead code — files and symbols with zero importers and no entry-point role. Uses the import graph to identify unreachable code. Returns confidence scores (1.0 = provably unreachable, 0.7 = all importers are themselves dead). Set granularity='file' for file-level results only.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

granularity.description: 'symbol' (default) returns dead symbols; 'file' returns dead files only.

min_confidence.description: Minimum confidence threshold 0.0–1.0. Default 0.8. Use 1.0 for provably unreachable only.

include_tests.description: Treat test files as live roots (default false — test files are excluded from dead code candidates).

entry_point_patterns.description: Additional glob patterns to treat as live roots (e.g. 'cli/*.py', 'scripts/*').

tools.name: get_ranked_context
tools.description: Assemble the best-fit context for a query within a token budget. Ranks all symbols by relevance (BM25) and/or centrality (PageRank), loads source for the top candidates, and packs greedily until token_budget is exhausted. Use when you want 'the best N tokens of context for this task' without specifying exact symbols.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

query.description: Natural language or identifier describing the task (max 500 chars)

strategy.description: 'combined' (default) = BM25 + PageRank weighted sum. 'bm25' = pure text relevance. 'centrality' = PageRank only, filtered to query-matching symbols.

include_kinds.description: Optional list of symbol kinds to restrict results (e.g. ['class', 'function']).

scope.description: Optional glob pattern to limit search to a subdirectory (e.g. 'src/core/*').

fusion.description: Enable multi-signal fusion (Weighted Reciprocal Rank) for ranking. Combines lexical, structural, and identity channels.

tools.name: get_changed_symbols
tools.description: Map a git diff to affected symbols: given two commits, returns which symbols were added, removed, modified, or renamed. Useful after merging a PR to answer 'what actually changed?' for code review or regression triage. Requires a locally indexed repo (index_folder). Defaults to comparing current HEAD against the SHA stored at index time.

repo.description: Repository identifier — must be locally indexed with index_folder

since_sha.description: Compare from this git SHA or ref. Defaults to the SHA stored at index time.

until_sha.description: Compare to this git SHA or ref (default 'HEAD').

include_blast_radius.description: Also return downstream importers (blast radius) for each changed symbol (default false).

tools.name: winnow_symbols
tools.description: Run a multi-axis constraint query against the index in a single round trip. Accepts an ordered list of criteria (AND) intersecting signals no other tool composes: kind, language, name (regex), file glob, cyclomatic complexity, decorator, direct call references, summary/docstring text, and git churn. Survivors are ranked by importance (PageRank, default), complexity, churn, or name. Use for questions like 'complex untested functions that call db.Exec' or 'deprecated methods still churning in the last 30 days' — cases that would otherwise require 4-5 separate calls and client-side merging.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

criteria.description: Ordered list of filters. Each item is {axis, op, value}. Supported axes: kind (in/eq), language (in/eq), name (eq/matches), file (matches - glob), complexity (>,<,>=,<=,==), decorator (contains), calls (contains - matches call_references), summary (contains), churn (>,<,>=,<=,== with optional window_days, default 90). All criteria must match (AND).

window_days.description: Only used when axis='churn'. Days of git history to scan (default 90).

tools.name: jcodemunch_guide
tools.description: Return the version-current CLAUDE.md / AGENT.md policy snippet for jcodemunch-mcp — the same text produced by `jcodemunch-mcp claude-md --generate`. Lets an agent keep a one-line CLAUDE.md (e.g. "Call jcodemunch_guide and strictly follow its instructions.") instead of pasting a static snippet that drifts from the installed version. Always force-included so it can't be hidden by disabled_tools or tier filtering. Idempotent, no repo context required.

tools.name: set_tool_tier
tools.description: Explicit tier override for the current session. Narrows or widens the exposed tool list to 'core' / 'standard' / 'full'. Prefer plan_turn(model=...) for routine per-task use; use set_tool_tier only when you need an explicit override (e.g. escalate mid-task to 'full' after a capability-gated failure).

tools.name: announce_model
tools.description: Agent self-reports its active model identifier. Server resolves to a tier via model_tier_map (fuzzy: normalize → exact → glob → substring → '*' → 'full') and narrows the exposed tool list accordingly. Idempotent: a second call with the same model is a cheap no-op. Prefer calling plan_turn(model=...) for routine per-task use; use announce_model as a fallback when plan_turn is not appropriate for the current task.

tools.name: index_local
tools.description: Index a local folder containing documentation files (.md, .txt, .rst). Parses by heading hierarchy into sections for efficient retrieval. Embeddings auto-enable when a provider is configured (GOOGLE_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, or sentence-transformers).

path.description: Path to local folder (absolute or relative, supports ~ for home directory)

use_ai_summaries.description: Use AI to generate section summaries (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or GOOGLE_API_KEY). When false, uses heading text.

use_embeddings.description: Generate semantic embeddings for each section, enabling hybrid (BM25+semantic) search. true/false/"auto". "auto" (default) enables embeddings when an embedding provider is configured (GOOGLE_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, or sentence-transformers installed).

extra_ignore_patterns.description: Additional gitignore-style patterns to exclude from indexing

follow_symlinks.description: Whether to follow symlinks. Default false for security.

max_files.description: Maximum number of doc files to index. Raise this for large doc trees. Default 500.

name.description: Optional repo identifier override. Use this when two folders share the same name (e.g. both named 'docs'). If omitted, the folder name is used. Example: 'requests-docs', 'flask-docs'.

incremental.description: When true (default), only re-index files that changed since the last index. Set to false to force a full re-index.

autotune.description: v1.29+ — when true, runs tune_weights against accumulated ranking events at the end of indexing. No-op when telemetry isn't enabled.

tools.name: doc_index_repo
tools.description: Index a GitHub repository's documentation. Fetches .md/.txt files, parses sections, and saves to local storage. Embeddings auto-enable when a provider is configured (GOOGLE_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, or sentence-transformers).

url.description: GitHub repository URL or owner/repo string

use_ai_summaries.description: Use AI to generate section summaries.

use_embeddings.description: Generate semantic embeddings for each section. true/false/"auto". "auto" (default) enables embeddings when an embedding provider is configured.

incremental.description: When true (default), skip all HTTP fetches if the HEAD commit SHA is unchanged; otherwise only re-index changed files. Set to false to force a full re-index.

tools.name: doc_list_repos
tools.description: List all indexed documentation repositories.

tools.name: get_index_overview
tools.description: v1.56+ — single-call repo snapshot: doc_count, section_count, total_byte_size, format_breakdown, top_tags, top_roles, indexed_at. Composition of v1.46/v1.50/v1.55 aggregations. Use for 'what is this repo at a glance?'

repo.description: Repository identifier

top_n.description: Top-N tags and roles to surface. 0 omits both lists; full distributions still available via get_all_tags / get_all_roles.

tools.name: get_doc
tools.description: v1.58+ — single-doc detail view. Pairs with list_docs (cross-doc inventory). Returns section list (handles), role_distribution, tag_distribution, byte_size, format, indexed_at for one doc. No content reads.

repo.description: Repository identifier

doc_path.description: Document path within the repo, e.g. 'api/auth.md'

tools.name: list_docs
tools.description: v1.55+ — flat per-doc inventory of an indexed repo: doc_path, section_count, format, byte_size for each indexed document. Lighter than get_toc_tree (which returns full section trees per doc). Sorted by doc_path.

repo.description: Repository identifier

tools.name: get_toc
tools.description: Get a flat table of contents for all sections in a repo, sorted by document order. Content is excluded — use get_section to retrieve content.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

path_glob.description: v1.36+ — fnmatch glob restricting results to matching doc_paths (e.g. 'api/**/*.md', 'reference/*'). Default: no filter.

tools.name: get_toc_tree
tools.description: Get a nested table of contents tree per document. Shows parent/child heading relationships. Content is excluded.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

path_glob.description: v1.36+ — fnmatch glob restricting results to matching doc_paths. Default: no filter.

tools.name: get_document_outline
tools.description: Get the section hierarchy for a single document file, without content.

repo.description: Repository identifier

doc_path.description: Path to the document within the repository (e.g., 'README.md')

tools.name: search_sections
tools.description: Search sections by relevance. Hybrid (BM25 lexical + semantic embedding) fusion when the index was built with use_embeddings=true; falls back to lexical-only otherwise. Returns summaries only — use get_section for full content.

repo.description: Repository identifier

doc_path.description: Optional: limit search to a specific document

path_glob.description: v1.36+ — fnmatch glob restricting results to matching doc_paths (e.g. 'api/**/*.md'). Stacks with doc_path.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

semantic.description: null/omit (auto — hybrid when embeddings exist), true (force hybrid), false (force lexical-only). Zero performance cost when the index has no embeddings.

semantic_only.description: Skip lexical scoring; rank purely by embedding cosine similarity.

semantic_weight.description: Weight (0.0–1.0) of semantic component in hybrid fusion. Lexical gets 1 - weight. Default 0.5.

role.description: Optional v1.19+ role filter. Values: concept, tutorial, how_to, reference, api, example, troubleshooting, changelog, faq, other.

profile.description: v1.32+ — task-aware retrieval profile. install/debug/explain/api each boost a small role set so matching sections rank ahead. Explicit role= overrides.

dedupe.description: v1.34+ — collapse near-duplicate sections to a single representative based on the v1.34 cluster sidecar. _meta.deduped reports suppressed member ids.

min_answerability.description: v1.42+ — drop results whose v1.33 _answerability score is below this threshold (0–1). _meta.quality_filtered reports drop count.

min_quotability.description: v1.42+ — drop results whose v1.33 _quotability score is below this threshold (0–1). Stacks with min_answerability.

min_level.description: v1.44+ — restrict to sections at heading level >= this. Inclusive.

max_level.description: v1.44+ — restrict to sections at heading level <= this. Inclusive. Stacks with min_level.

tags.description: v1.45+ — restrict to sections whose Section.tags contains every listed tag (AND semantics). Case-insensitive.

exclude_tags.description: v1.51+ — drop sections whose Section.tags contains ANY listed tag (negative ANY-match). Stacks with `tags`. Case-insensitive.

roles.description: v1.52+ — restrict to sections whose metadata.role matches ANY listed role (positive OR-match). Differs from singular `role` (which is exact). Case-insensitive.

exclude_roles.description: v1.52+ — drop sections whose metadata.role matches ANY listed role. Case-insensitive. Stacks with `roles` (the result must match an included role and not match any excluded role).

min_byte_length.description: v1.53+ — drop sections shorter than this many bytes (byte_end - byte_start). Use to filter out stubs / one-liners.

max_byte_length.description: v1.53+ — drop sections longer than this many bytes. Use to filter out oversized dumps. Stacks with min_byte_length.

repo_group.description: v1.26+ — fan out across the named repo group (defined via define_repo_group). When set, the per-repo `repo` arg is ignored; results from each member repo are fused via RRF.

tools.name: search_titles
tools.description: v1.57+ — fast title-only token-overlap match. Different from search_sections (full hybrid retrieval). Use for navigation: 'find the section whose heading text matches X'. Handle-only output ({id, title, level, doc_path, _score}); no content reads, no embeddings.

repo.description: Repository identifier

tools.name: get_section
tools.description: Retrieve the full content of a specific section using byte-range reads. Use after identifying section IDs via search_sections or get_toc.

repo.description: Repository identifier

section_id.description: Section ID from get_toc, search_sections, or get_document_outline

verify.description: Verify content hash matches stored hash (detects source drift)

strip_boilerplate.description: v1.24+ — when true, suppress repeated cross-section fragments (footers, nav, license headers) before returning content.

compress_code.description: v1.35+ — when true, drop blank lines and full-line comments inside fenced code blocks before returning. _meta.code_compressed_bytes reports bytes saved.

tools.name: get_sections
tools.description: Batch content retrieval for multiple sections in one call.

repo.description: Repository identifier

section_ids.description: List of section IDs to retrieve

strip_boilerplate.description: v1.24+ — strip repeated cross-section fragments per section before returning.

compress_code.description: v1.35+ — drop blank lines and full-line comments inside fenced code blocks before returning. _meta.code_compressed_bytes reports total bytes saved.

tools.name: get_section_context
tools.description: Retrieve a section with its full hierarchy context: ancestor headings (root → parent) for orientation, the target section's content, and immediate child summaries. Prevents 'section too thin' without falling back to whole-file reads.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

section_id.description: Target section ID from get_toc, search_sections, etc.

max_tokens.description: Approximate token budget for the target section's content (bytes/4 estimate). Ancestors and child summaries are always included.

include_children.description: Include immediate child section summaries (no content reads). Default true.

include_related.description: v1.20+ adaptive context: append structural + semantic neighbor summaries.

strip_boilerplate.description: v1.24+ — strip repeated cross-section fragments before returning the target section content.

tools.name: describe_section
tools.description: v1.54+ — consolidated handle bundle: full metadata + ancestor breadcrumb + prev/next/parent/first_child neighbors for one section in a single call. Saves three round-trips vs calling get_section_summary + get_section_path + section_neighbors separately. No content reads.

repo.description: Repository identifier

tools.name: section_neighbors
tools.description: v1.37+ — return prev/next siblings (in document order), parent, and first child for a section. Handles only (id, title, level, doc_path) — no content. Use for fast sequential navigation without re-querying search_sections.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

section_id.description: Target section ID from get_toc, search_sections, etc.

tools.name: get_recent_changes
tools.description: v1.47+ — list sections whose source has drifted from index state (edited_uncommitted or stale_index buckets via the v1.16 FreshnessProbe). Pre-flight check before deciding whether to re-index. Handle-only — no content reads.

repo.description: Repository identifier

include_stale.description: Include sections in stale_index bucket (byte range no longer hashes the same).

include_edited.description: Include sections in edited_uncommitted bucket (file changed but this section's range still matches).

tools.name: get_all_roles
tools.description: v1.50+ — list every distinct role classification across the repo with per-role section counts and id samples. Companion to v1.46 get_all_tags. Sections without metadata.role are bucketed under 'unknown'. Use to discover what roles exist before constructing a `role=` or `profile=` query.

repo.description: Repository identifier

sample_size.description: How many section_ids to surface per role. 0 omits samples.

tools.name: get_all_tags
tools.description: v1.46+ — list every unique #hashtag across the repo with per-tag section counts. Companion to the v1.45 `tags` filter on search_sections — use this to discover what tag namespaces exist before constructing a tag-filtered query. Lowercase-normalized.

repo.description: Repository identifier

min_section_count.description: Drop tags appearing in fewer than this many sections (filter out typos).

tools.name: get_section_descendants
tools.description: v1.43+ — return every descendant of a section (BFS over parent_id) in document order with depth offset. Pairs with get_section_path (ancestors). Optional max_depth caps the walk; max_depth=1 returns immediate children only. Handles only — no content.

repo.description: Repository identifier

section_id.description: Target section. Its descendants are returned; target itself is not included.

max_depth.description: Optional cap on traversal depth. None = full subtree. 1 = immediate children only.

tools.name: get_section_excerpts
tools.description: v1.49+ — batch counterpart to get_section_excerpt. Resolves N previews in one call against a single index load. Per-id errors reported in-line. _meta.tokens_saved aggregates byte savings across the batch.

repo.description: Repository identifier

section_ids.description: List of section IDs. Order preserved; each entry carries `requested_id` for correlation.

max_bytes.description: Per-section soft cap in UTF-8 bytes.

tools.name: get_section_excerpt
tools.description: v1.41+ — return a short content preview (default 500 bytes) for one section. Trimmed to last newline before the cap so it ends on a paragraph boundary. Use to peek at content before paying for a full get_section read. _meta.tokens_saved reports the byte-savings vs full content.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

max_bytes.description: Soft cap on excerpt size in UTF-8 bytes. Default 500.

tools.name: get_section_path
tools.description: v1.40+ — return the breadcrumb chain (root → ... → target) for a section_id. Walks parent_id upward; cycle-protected. Handles only ({id, title, level, doc_path}) per step plus depth.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

section_id.description: Target section ID from get_toc, search_sections, etc.

tools.name: get_orphan_sections
tools.description: v1.39+ — list sections whose doc_path receives zero inbound references from any other doc. Companion to get_broken_links and get_stale_pages: documentation that exists but nobody links to.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

include_same_doc.description: If true, count intra-document anchor links as inbound (e.g. a TOC at the top of a page). Default false — only cross-document references count.

tools.name: get_section_summary
tools.description: v1.38+ — return full indexed metadata (title, summary, role, tags, metadata, parent_id, children, content_hash, byte_start/end, byte_length) for one section without fetching content. Use to inspect role/tags before deciding whether to read the content via get_section.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

section_id.description: Target section ID from get_toc, search_sections, etc.

tools.name: get_section_summaries
tools.description: v1.48+ — batch version of get_section_summary. Resolve metadata for many ids in one call against a single index load. Per-id errors are reported in-line on the corresponding result entry rather than aborting the batch.

repo.description: Repository identifier

section_ids.description: List of section IDs to look up. Order preserved in response; each entry carries `requested_id` for correlation.

tools.name: delete_index
tools.description: Remove a repo index and its cached raw files.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

tools.name: get_broken_links
tools.description: Scan indexed doc files for internal cross-references that no longer resolve. Checks markdown links, RST :ref:/:doc: directives, and anchor-only links (#heading). External links (http/https) are skipped. Output: list of {source_file, source_section, target, reason} where reason is 'file_not_found', 'section_not_found', or 'anchor_not_found'.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

tools.name: get_doc_coverage
tools.description: Check which jcodemunch symbols have matching documentation in this doc index. Given a list of jcodemunch symbol IDs, reports which symbols are mentioned in section titles (documented) vs absent (undocumented). Bridges jcodemunch <-> jdocmunch. symbol_ids capped at 200. Output: {documented, undocumented, coverage_pct}.

repo.description: Doc repo identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

symbol_ids.description: List of jcodemunch symbol IDs to check coverage for

tools.name: get_backlinks
tools.description: Find all sections that link TO a given document (inverse reference graph). Useful for the LLM Wiki pattern: when a source changes, find which wiki pages reference it. Output: list of {source_file, source_section, source_section_id, link}.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

doc_path.description: Target document path to find backlinks for (e.g., 'raw/article.md' or 'wiki/concepts/auth.md')

tools.name: get_stale_pages
tools.description: Find wiki pages whose declared sources have been modified on disk. Convention: wiki pages include YAML frontmatter with a 'sources' list of relative paths to raw source files. This tool checks whether those source files have changed since the page was last indexed. Output: list of {doc_path, title, stale_sources} where each stale source has a reason: 'modified', 'missing', or 'untracked'.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

sources_dir.description: Base directory for resolving relative source paths. If omitted, uses the index's source_root.

tools.name: get_wiki_stats
tools.description: Wiki health dashboard. Returns: orphan pages (zero inbound internal links), most-linked pages (top 10), tag distribution, total internal link count, and sections-per-doc min/max/avg. Use for periodic wiki lint checks.

repo.description: Repository identifier (owner/repo or just repo name)

tools.name: analyze_perf
tools.description: Per-tool latency analysis. window='session' reads the in-memory ring (last 512 calls per tool — always available); window='1h'|'24h'|'7d'|'all' reads the persistent SQLite sink at ~/.doc-index/telemetry.db (opt-in via JDOCMUNCH_PERF_TELEMETRY=1). Returns {window, telemetry_enabled, source, per_tool:{tool:{count,p50_ms,p95_ms,max_ms,errors,error_rate}}}.

window.description: Time window. 'session' uses the in-memory ring; longer windows require JDOCMUNCH_PERF_TELEMETRY=1.

tools.name: get_session_stats
tools.description: Session self-monitor: returns {latency_per_tool, total_tokens_saved}. Lightweight; reads the in-memory latency ring + persistent savings counter. For windowed analysis use analyze_perf.

tools.name: find_code_examples
tools.description: Search fenced code blocks across the indexed docs by BM25 over the block content. Returns one row per block with {block_id, section_id, doc_path, title, lang, byte_start, byte_end, snippet, _score}. Optional lang filter (e.g. 'python', 'bash'). Use after index_local; requires INDEX_VERSION>=3.

repo.description: jdocmunch repo identifier

tools.name: link_code_to_symbols
tools.description: Best-effort bridge from doc code blocks to jcodemunch code symbols. For each block, tokenizes identifiers and looks them up via jcodemunch's search_symbols. Returns {by_block, by_symbol, _meta} where _meta.bridge_available reports whether jcodemunch-mcp is importable.

repo.description: jdocmunch repo identifier

tools.name: find_endpoint
tools.description: Find OpenAPI operations by path glob, method, and/or tag. All filters AND'd. Returns one row per match with {section_id, doc_path, method, path, operationId, summary, tags, deprecated}. Requires the spec to have been indexed under v1.18+ so structured metadata is present.

tools.name: list_endpoints_by_tag
tools.description: Return every operation whose tags list contains the given tag (exact). Convenience wrapper around find_endpoint with only a tag filter.

tools.name: find_operations_using_schema
tools.description: Return every operation whose request body or any response references the given schema. Each row gets a referenced_in list of all schema names that operation pulls in (so you can see the broader dependency cluster).

tools.name: get_schema_graph
tools.description: BFS walk of the schema reference graph from a root schema name. Returns {root, nodes:{name:{type, properties, required, refs}}, edges:[[from, to]], unresolved}. max_depth bounds the walk (default 5).

tools.name: lookup_term
tools.description: Glossary lookup. Returns every entry whose term equals the query (case-insensitive, exact). Glossary entries are extracted at index time from **Term** — definition Markdown patterns and RST .. glossary:: blocks.

tools.name: list_terms
tools.description: List glossary terms in alphabetical order, optionally filtered by prefix. Capped at max_results (default 100).

tools.name: get_related_sections
tools.description: v2.0+ related-section graph. Returns structural neighbors (siblings, children, parent, optional cousins) and semantic neighbors (top-N cosine over stored embeddings, score >= min_score). mode: structural | semantic | both.

tools.name: get_section_diff
tools.description: Unified diff between the indexed snapshot and the current on-disk byte range for a section. Returns hashes + diff text; identical=true when the section is in sync with disk.

tools.name: get_doc_health
tools.description: One-shot index health diagnostics. Returns section_count, doc_count, role_distribution, freshness counts, broken_link_count, drift status, BM25 corpus sanity, and embedding coverage.

tools.name: get_tutorial_path
tools.description: Reconstruct an ordered tutorial chain starting from section_id. Detects frontmatter next:/prev: keys, inline 'Next:' / 'Previous:' markdown links, or ordered numeric filename prefixes (01-intro.md). Returns chain[] of {section_id, doc_path, title} plus the strategy used.

tools.name: get_undocumented_symbols
tools.description: Best-effort inverse coverage: enumerate symbols in the jcodemunch code_repo and return those whose name (or qualified name) does not appear anywhere in this doc index. _meta.bridge_available=false when jcodemunch-mcp is not importable in this environment.

tools.name: list_repo_groups
tools.description: List defined repo groups (v1.26+). Each group is a named alias for a set of indexed repos that search_sections can fan out across via the repo_group kwarg.

tools.name: define_repo_group
tools.description: Create, replace, or delete a repo group (v1.26+). Empty repos list deletes the group. Persisted to ~/.doc-index/_groups.jsonc (JSONC — hand-edits welcome).

tools.name: tune_weights
tools.description: Online weight tuning. Reads ranking_events from ~/.doc-index/telemetry.db (requires JDOCMUNCH_PERF_TELEMETRY=1) and proposes a per-repo semantic_weight step. dry_run=true skips the disk write. min_events gates against early overfitting.

repo.description: Optional — single repo to tune. Omit to scan all repos with events.

tools.name: verify_index
tools.description: Byte-offset integrity check. Walks every section, byte-range-reads its current on-disk content, recomputes SHA-256, and compares to the stored content_hash. Reports drift / missing / error counts plus the drifting section ids. Sample N sections via the sample arg for cheap CI checks.

sample.description: Only verify the first N sections.

tools.name: check_embedding_drift
tools.description: Embedding-drift canary. Without args, re-embeds the saved CANARY_STRINGS and reports per-canary cosine drift; alarm fires when max_drift > threshold (default 0.05 ≈ cosine<0.95). Pass capture=true to seed the snapshot first (idempotent unless force=true). Catches silent provider model upgrades that would otherwise corrupt index recall without changing dim.

capture.description: Embed CANARY_STRINGS and persist the snapshot.

force.description: With capture=true, overwrite an existing snapshot.
